data.title: Metaplanet releases Q1 financial report: Thanks to Bitcoin reserve strategy, the company's quarterly performance is the best in history
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: Metaplanet, a Japanese listed company, released its Q1 financial report this morning. The financial report pointed out that Metaplanet achieved the best performance in the company's history this quarter, with revenue of US$6.139 million, an increase of 8% month-on-month and a surge of 943% year-on-year, and profit of US$4.151 million, an increase of 11% month-on-month, of which 88% of the revenue came from Bitcoin option sales.
In addition, the company currently holds a total of 6,796 Bitcoins, with a goal of reaching a reserve of 10,000 Bitcoins by the end of the year.

data.title: Lawyer: If the US Congress fails to pass the GENIUS Act in time, crypto regulatory reform will be delayed until 2029
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: John E Deaton, a lawyer who supports cryptocurrencies, warned that if the US Congress fails to pass the stablecoin GENIUS Act in time, substantive crypto regulatory reforms may be delayed until 2029. This view is supported by Messari founder Ryan Selkis and others. Deaton criticized the US regulatory attitude for swinging dramatically between government changes, and the current regulation still relies on the legal framework of the 1930s and 1940s. Selkis specifically pointed out that if the bill is not passed this week, crypto reforms may die during the Trump administration in the future. (Coinpedia)

data.title: Bipartisan Senators Near Consensus on GENIUS Act
data.description: According to BlockBeats, a bipartisan group of U.S. senators is nearing a consensus on the text of the GENIUS Act, although the content has not been finalized. The Senate is currently discussing the resumption of the bill's review process, aiming to complete the vote before the Memorial Day recess. However, progress depends on reaching a procedural agreement with the Democratic Party.

data.title: Nebraska passes new bill to make Bitcoin mining more difficult
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News The Nebraska State Legislature just passed a bill to regulate Bitcoin miners. LB 526 requires large miners to bear the cost of infrastructure upgrades, requires miners to report energy usage, and allows authorities to interrupt service. The bill was passed unanimously with a vote of 49:0. 
Nebraska's Bitcoin mining bill LB 526 is not a huge setback for companies in the state. The bill is just over two pages long and is very straightforward. Unlike the anti-mining bill, it does not involve carbon emissions or other environmental impacts. Instead, it focuses almost entirely on the power grid.

data.title: Nebraska Legislature Passes Bitcoin Mining Regulation Bill
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the Nebraska Legislature has unanimously passed a bill regulating Bitcoin miners. The LB 526 bill requires large-scale miners to cover infrastructure upgrade costs, report energy usage, and grants authorities the power to interrupt services. The bill was approved with a 49:0 vote.

The LB 526 Bitcoin mining bill is not considered a significant setback for companies in Nebraska. The legislation is concise, spanning just over two pages, and is straightforward in its approach. Unlike anti-mining bills, it does not address carbon emissions or other environmental impacts, focusing instead almost entirely on the electrical grid.

data.title: XRP Frenzy Builds: Over $1 Billion in Open Interest Signals Breakout Tension
data.description:

data.title: Ethereum Foundation aims to secure the future with trillion-dollar security initiative
data.description:
The Ethereum Foundation (EF) has unveiled the Trillion-Dollar Security (1TS) initiative, a long-term plan to raise Ethereum’s security standards in line with its growing importance in global finance. The Foundation said it aims to position Ethereum as a network capable of securely holding trillions of dollars in assets for individuals and institutions.
source: https://cryptoslate.com/ethereum-foundation-aims-to-secure-the-future-with-trillion-dollar-security-initiative/

data.title: Polymarket's new market creation hits record high in April, number of active traders drops
data.description: According to data from The Block, Polymarket's new market creation reached an all-time high in April, with more than 7,000 prediction markets launched, up 20.7% from 5,800 in March. Despite the surge in market creation, the number of active traders has dropped from 462,000 in January to 330,000 in April, and trading volume has stabilized, with total bets of less than $900 million in April, down from $2.6 billion in January. The report said this may indicate that the user base is shifting toward more centralized market creators.

data.title: Director of the U.S. Digital Assets Committee: It is unfair to question the Trump family's involvement in the crypto industry
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: At the Consensus 2025 conference held in Toronto, Robert Hines (Bo Hines), executive director of the US Presidential Digital Asset Advisory Committee, said that the questioning of the Trump family's involvement in the cryptocurrency industry was "unfair" and criticized the Biden administration's crackdown on the crypto industry as an "unprecedented war." He pointed out that the current government will be committed to clearing "Operation Chokepoint 2.0" regulatory barriers, promoting the free interaction between traditional finance and crypto assets, and emphasized that the White House's establishment of a full-time crypto affairs commissioner is an important policy turning point.
